task: I need a simple way to clean up a growing pile of project files before it turns into a mess. Can you help me set up a folder and naming approach that makes it easier to find the right version later and keep exports straight?

turns.agent_response:
Yes ‚Äî keep it simple and consistent. The goal is to make **current / old / export** obvious at a glance.

## Folder structure
Use one main project folder with a few stable subfolders:

- `01_Project`
- `02_Media`
- `03_Exports`
- `04_Notes`
- `99_Archive`

If you need more detail, add subfolders inside those, but don‚Äôt overbuild it.

### Example
- `01_Project/`
  - project files
  - sequences
- `02_Media/`
  - camera originals
  - audio
  - graphics
- `03_Exports/`
  - review exports
  - finals
  - delivery versions
- `04_Notes/`
  - client notes
  - change log
- `99_Archive/`
  - old versions
  - superseded exports
  - discarded tests

## Naming rule
Use a consistent pattern:

**ProjectName_v###_status_date**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v012_review_2026-04-29`
- `ClientSpot_v013_locked_2026-04-29`
- `ClientSpot_v013_final_2026-04-29`
- `ClientSpot_v013_tv_master_2026-04-29`

## What to include in names
- project name
- version number
- status
- date
- delivery type if needed

## Simple versioning rule
- bump the version number for any meaningful change
- don‚Äôt reuse old numbers
- don‚Äôt call anything ‚Äúfinal‚Äù unless it really is final
- use `review`, `locked`, `final`, `archive`, or `delivery` to show status

## Export naming
Keep exports obvious:
- `ProjectName_v012_review.mp4`
- `ProjectName_v013_web.mp4`
- `ProjectName_v013_tv.mov`

## Good habits
- archive old versions instead of deleting them
- keep one current working file
- don‚Äôt mix export tests with finals
- keep notes in one place

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page folder/naming template** you can copy into your workflow.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close. I‚Äôd want it a little tighter for post work ‚Äî especially so exports don‚Äôt get mixed with project files.

Can you make it more specific for a real edit job, like:
- where the working project file lives
- where review exports go vs finals
- how to mark client notes or revision rounds
- whether version numbers reset per job or keep climbing inside the job

Also, I‚Äôd rather not have too many folders up front. Two or three levels max unless there‚Äôs a reason.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely ‚Äî here‚Äôs a tighter post-work version with fewer folders and clearer separation.

## Simple post folder structure

**Top level**
- `01_Project`
- `02_Media`
- `03_Exports`
- `04_Notes`
- `99_Archive`

## Where things live

### `01_Project`
Put the **working project file** here:
- current edit project
- autosaves if your app uses them
- any sequence/project backups you want to keep active

### `02_Media`
Put **source and working media** here:
- camera originals
- audio
- graphics
- temp assets
- linked files

### `03_Exports`
Split exports by purpose:
- `Review`
- `Final`
- `Delivery`

Example:
- `03_Exports/Review`
- `03_Exports/Final`
- `03_Exports/Delivery`

### `04_Notes`
Keep all notes and revision tracking here:
- client notes
- internal notes
- revision log
- approvals

### `99_Archive`
Move old versions here:
- old project files
- superseded exports
- obsolete notes
- tests you no longer need active

---

## Naming rule

Use:

**ProjectName_v###_status**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v001_work`
- `ClientSpot_v002_review`
- `ClientSpot_v003_locked`
- `ClientSpot_v003_final`
- `ClientSpot_v003_tv_delivery`

---

## Versioning rule

### Keep climbing inside the job
- Don‚Äôt reset version numbers mid-project
- Keep one running sequence of versions until the job is done
- If the job restarts later, start a new version chain only if it‚Äôs a new project or a clearly separate phase

### Simple rule
- **One job = one version series**
- **One meaningful change = next version**
- **No reused numbers**

---

## Notes / revision rounds

Keep notes in one file or folder inside `04_Notes`:
- `ClientSpot_notes_v003`
- `ClientSpot_revisions_round1`
- `ClientSpot_change_log`

If you want to track rounds, use:
- `round1`
- `round2`
- `round3`

That keeps client feedback separate from the edit files.

---

## Export naming

Keep exports clearly separated by type:
- `ClientSpot_v003_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot_v003_final.mov`
- `ClientSpot_v003_tv_delivery.mov`

If needed, include the delivery target:
- `ClientSpot_v003_web.mp4`
- `ClientSpot_v003_tv.mov`

---

## Simple rule of thumb
- **Project files** stay in `01_Project`
- **Exports** stay in `03_Exports`
- **Notes** stay in `04_Notes`
- **Old stuff** goes in `99_Archive`

If you want, I can turn this into a **very short copy-paste folder template** with example filenames for a real job.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, this is closer. Still want it a bit more practical, though.

What I‚Äôd use on an actual job:

- one active project file in `01_Project`
- review exports in `03_Exports/Review`
- finals in `03_Exports/Final`
- delivery-specific masters in `03_Exports/Delivery` if needed
- notes in one file under `04_Notes`, with revision rounds clearly labeled

On versioning: keep it climbing through the job. Don‚Äôt reset it every time the client has notes. So `v001`, `v002`, `v003`... all the way through that job. If it‚Äôs a new job, start over at `v001` for that project. That part is cleaner.

For notes, I‚Äôd probably do something like:
- `ClientSpot_notes_v003`
- `ClientSpot_rev_round1`
- `ClientSpot_rev_round2`

or one running change log if it‚Äôs easier.

Main thing I‚Äôd still want is the exact naming pattern for exports vs project files so there‚Äôs no chance of mixing them up. Like, should the working project file say `work`, `edit`, or `project`? And should finals use `final` only once it‚Äôs actually locked, or do you still keep `review` vs `final` all the way through? I‚Äôm trying to keep it tight and not make extra folders just to solve naming mistakes.

turns.agent_response:
Yes ‚Äî here‚Äôs the tighter version with a clear naming pattern.

## Folder structure
Keep it simple:

- `01_Project`
- `02_Media`
- `03_Exports`
  - `Review`
  - `Final`
  - `Delivery`
- `04_Notes`
- `99_Archive`

---

## Naming pattern

### Working project file
Use:
**ProjectName_v###_work**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v001_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot_v002_work.aep`
- `ClientSpot_v003_work.drp`

Use `work` for the active editable project file.  
That keeps it obvious it‚Äôs not a final export.

### Review exports
Use:
**ProjectName_v###_review**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot_v002_review.mov`

Use `review` for anything going to the client before approval.

### Final exports
Use:
**ProjectName_v###_final**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v003_final.mp4`
- `ClientSpot_v004_final.mov`

Use `final` only when the cut is actually locked or approved.

### Delivery masters
Use:
**ProjectName_v###_delivery_target**

Examples:
- `ClientSpot_v004_delivery_web.mp4`
- `ClientSpot_v004_delivery_tv.mov`

Use this when you need a spec-specific master.

---

## Versioning rule
- Keep the version number climbing through the job
- Don‚Äôt reset it for client notes
- One job = one version series
- New job = start back at `v001`

---

## Notes
Keep one notes file in `04_Notes`:
- `ClientSpot_notes_v003`
- `ClientSpot_change_log_v003`
- `ClientSpot_rev_round1`
- `ClientSpot_rev_round2`

If you want the simplest setup, use just one running file:
- `ClientSpot_change_log_v003`

---

## Simple rule
- **work** = editable project file
- **review** = client-facing check export
- **final** = approved cut
- **delivery** = spec-specific master

If you want, I can make this into a **copy-paste template** with sample filenames for Premiere, Avid, and Resolve.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that‚Äôs the right shape. I‚Äôd tighten one thing though: I‚Äôd rather not use `final` too early. In real work I‚Äôd keep `review` until it‚Äôs actually approved, then `final` only for the locked version.

So I‚Äôd use:
- working project: `ProjectName_v###_work`
- client review: `ProjectName_v###_review`
- approved/locked export: `ProjectName_v###_final`
- spec master: `ProjectName_v###_delivery_web` / `..._tv`

For the project file itself, `work` feels cleaner than `edit` or `project`. `project` is too generic, and `edit` can get fuzzy once you have exports sitting —Ä—è–¥–æ–º with it. `work` makes the active file obvious.

For notes, one running change log is probably enough. Something like:
- `ClientSpot_change_log_v003`
- inside it: `Round 1`, `Round 2`, `Round 3`

That keeps the folder count down.

One thing I‚Äôd still want from you: a dead-simple example of a full job tree, with one actual sequence of filenames from v001 through delivery. Like, show me the whole path for a typical job so I can copy the pattern without thinking about it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely ‚Äî here‚Äôs a dead-simple example you can copy.

## Example job tree

**Project folder**
- `ClientSpot/`

**Inside**
- `01_Project/`
  - `ClientSpot_v001_work.prproj`
  - `ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj`
  - `ClientSpot_v003_work.prproj`
  - `ClientSpot_v004_work.prproj`
  - `ClientSpot_v005_work.prproj`
- `02_Media/`
  - source media, audio, graphics
- `03_Exports/`
  - `Review/`
    - `ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`
    - `ClientSpot_v002_review.mp4`
    - `ClientSpot_v003_review.mp4`
  - `Final/`
    - `ClientSpot_v004_final.mp4`
  - `Delivery/`
    - `ClientSpot_v004_delivery_web.mp4`
    - `ClientSpot_v004_delivery_tv.mov`
- `04_Notes/`
  - `ClientSpot_change_log_v003`
- `99_Archive/`
  - old project files
  - superseded exports

## How it works
- `v001_work` through `v003_work` = active edit versions
- `v003_review` = client-facing review export for that stage
- `v004_final` = approved/locked export
- `v004_delivery_web` and `v004_delivery_tv` = delivery-specific masters from the approved version

## Simple rule
- **work** = editable project file
- **review** = client check export
- **final** = approved locked export
- **delivery** = spec-specific master

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page copy-paste template** with blanks you can reuse for any job.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah ‚Äî this is basically it. Just want the copy-paste version with the paths filled in cleanly, no extra fluff.

Like:
- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v001_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Final/ClientSpot_v004_final.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Delivery/ClientSpot_v004_delivery_web.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/04_Notes/ClientSpot_change_log_v003`

And maybe one note on the archive side ‚Äî whether I‚Äôd move old project files there as `ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj` or rename them as archived, like `ClientSpot_v002_work_archive.prproj`. I‚Äôm fine either way, but I want one rule.

If you give me that, I can just use it.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely ‚Äî here‚Äôs the copy-paste version with one archive rule.

## Copy-paste template

- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v001_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v003_work.prproj`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v002_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v003_review.mp4`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Final/ClientSpot_v004_final.mp4`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Delivery/ClientSpot_v004_delivery_web.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Delivery/ClientSpot_v004_delivery_tv.mov`

- `ClientSpot/04_Notes/ClientSpot_change_log_v003`

## Archive rule
Use **archive as-is** in `99_Archive`:
- `ClientSpot/99_Archive/ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/99_Archive/ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`

Don‚Äôt rename them to `_archive` unless you need an extra label for your own workflow.  
One rule is cleaner: **move old versions into `99_Archive` and keep the filename unchanged**.

## Simple rule
- active work stays in `01_Project`
- client checks stay in `03_Exports/Review`
- approved locked export stays in `03_Exports/Final`
- delivery masters stay in `03_Exports/Delivery`
- old versions move to `99_Archive`

If you want, I can also give you the same thing as a **blank template** with `ProjectName` placeholders instead of `ClientSpot`.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that‚Äôs usable.

One rule for archive is fine: move old versions into `99_Archive` and keep the filename unchanged. No `_archive` suffix.

Copy-paste version I‚Äôd actually use:

- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v001_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/01_Project/ClientSpot_v003_work.prproj`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v002_review.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Review/ClientSpot_v003_review.mp4`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Final/ClientSpot_v004_final.mp4`

- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Delivery/ClientSpot_v004_delivery_web.mp4`
- `ClientSpot/03_Exports/Delivery/ClientSpot_v004_delivery_tv.mov`

- `ClientSpot/04_Notes/ClientSpot_change_log_v003`

- `ClientSpot/99_Archive/ClientSpot_v002_work.prproj`
- `ClientSpot/99_Archive/ClientSpot_v001_review.mp4`

That‚Äôs clean enough. No extra naming games.
